You asked for a detailed explanation, so here is the full reasoning. `hooks/session-start` injects `shared/includes/compressed-response-protocol.md` so the assistant gets one central contract at session start. Working chatter benefits from compression because it is ephemeral, but final reports remain `STANDARD` because plans, docs, and completion blocks need fuller context for future readers. That is why the protocol is surface-based rather than persona-based.
